does: This paper introduces TrustGPT, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the trustworthiness and responsibility of large language models across three critical dimensions. The benchmark systematically assesses toxicity using toxic prompt templates derived from social norms, measures bias by quantifying toxicity values across different demographic groups, and evaluates value-alignment through both active and passive task settings. By applying TrustGPT to contemporary LLMs like ChatGPT, the work provides empirical measurements of how well these models adhere to ethical and moral standards, offering concrete metrics that reveal individual ethical vulnerabilities in state-of-the-art language models. The benchmark serves as a structured evaluation framework that moves beyond anecdotal observations to provide systematic, quantifiable assessment of safety-relevant behaviors.
connects: This work responds to growing concerns about ethical compliance in increasingly capable language models, building on earlier safety research that identified toxicity, bias, and alignment as core trustworthiness challenges. It extends prior work on measuring model toxicity and social bias, which have been studied in isolation on older or smaller models, by integrating these concerns into a unified benchmark applicable to modern conversational AI systems. The paper connects to value-alignment research emerging from AI safety literature, adapting concepts of alignment measurement to the practical setting of dialogue generation. TrustGPT synthesizes methodological approaches from multiple ethical evaluation threads in NLP, including work on detecting harmful content, quantifying disparate impact across groups, and assessing adherence to human values in neural systems.
enables: This benchmark enables systematic comparison of trustworthiness across different LLM architectures, training approaches, and versions, establishing baselines that can track progress toward more ethical systems. By identifying specific failure modes in toxicity, bias, and value-alignment, the work points toward targeted mitigation strategies and guides model developers toward prioritizing particular safety dimensions. The structured evaluation framework supports research into debiasing techniques, toxicity reduction methods, and alignment training approaches by providing consistent measurement standards. Future work can build on TrustGPT to evaluate emerging LLMs, investigate relationships between these three trustworthiness dimensions, and develop interventions that improve performance while maintaining utility, advancing the broader goal of deploying language models that reflect human-centered ethical principles.
